findings.title: Venue adapters are duplicated shims over a non-venue executor interface
findings.suggested_fix: Either implement each adapter against its real venue interface and payload schema, or collapse the four duplicated libraries into a single explicitly named scaffold adapter and move IRouterLegExecutor into test/mock-only code. As written, Solidly/UniswapV2/Slipstream/Universal all call target.executeLeg(...), while the real venue interfaces are unused, which makes the production trust boundary around leg.target look venue-specific when it is actually an arbitrary enabled executor contract.

findings.title: Unused WETH state makes wrapping support look implemented
findings.suggested_fix: Remove weth from the constructor/deploy script until the router actually wraps or unwraps, or wire it through explicit native/WETH conversion paths with tests. The current immutable is set from BASE_WETH_ADDRESS but never read, which adds audit noise and can make integrators assume native/WETH bridge behavior exists.

findings.title: Dispatch defaults unknown or future venue families to UniversalRouter
findings.suggested_fix: Make UniswapV3 and UniswapV4 explicit branches and add an UnknownVenueFamily-style revert for the fallback. This keeps future enum additions or refactors from silently inheriting the UniversalRouter adapter path after only passing the generic family/target allowlist checks.

findings.title: Route parameter is carried through execution only for a no-op
findings.suggested_fix: Drop the route parameter from _executeLeg and remove the trailing route; statement/comment. The validation-before-execution invariant is already visible at the executeRoute call site, and the no-op dependency adds bytecode/cognitive noise without enforcing anything.

residual_risks: I did not run the test suite; this persona pass was read-only aside from the requested artifact.
The production fixture snapshot and live venue payload formats are still pending, so adapter-level auditability depends on follow-up implementation rather than the current unit-test mocks.
testing_gaps: No tests exercise real Solidly, UniswapV2, Slipstream, UniswapV3, or UniswapV4 router calls; current adapter coverage only proves the IRouterLegExecutor mock path.
No tests cover any intended use of the constructor WETH address, because no router code currently reads it.
